task_completion.description: Drawio application is open with a document containing at least two pages.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Drawio interface with page tabs (usually at the bottom) showing at least two distinct pages.

task_completion.description: Page 1 is titled 'Triage Algorithm' and contains a flowchart with decision nodes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the title 'Triage Algorithm' on the first page and a flowchart structure containing at least 3 diamond shapes (decision points) and multiple connected nodes.

task_completion.description: Page 1 flowchart nodes are color-coded RED, ORANGE, and GREEN based on risk levels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for flowchart nodes filled with red (for high risk/STEMI/NSTEMI), orange (for moderate risk/Observation), and green (for low risk/Discharge).

task_completion.description: Page 2 is titled 'HEART Score' and contains a reference table with five components.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the second page tab titled 'HEART Score' containing a table or structured list detailing the 5 components of the HEART score.

task_completion.description: The diagram is saved as 'chest_pain_triage.drawio' on the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Drawio window title bar showing 'chest_pain_triage.drawio' or the file visible on the Desktop / in the file manager.

task_completion.description: Page 1 is exported as 'chest_pain_triage.png' on the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an export dialog confirming the PNG save, or the 'chest_pain_triage.png' file visible on the Desktop or in the file manager.

integrity.description: The agent used the Drawio GUI to manually create the flowchart.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the Drawio application open with toolbars, shape libraries, and active selection/editing of shapes, rather than just terminal commands generating the file.

integrity.description: The flowchart consists of genuine Drawio shapes and connectors, not a pasted image.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for individual selectable shapes, grid lines, and routing waypoints on connector lines indicating the diagram was built component by component.

integrity.description: The agent extracted clinical logic and HEART score components from the provided text file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or terminal window open showing the contents of 'chest_pain_pathway.txt' being read or referenced during the creation process.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input file is located at /home/ga/Desktop/chest_pain_pathway.txt. The output files must be saved exactly as /home/ga/Desktop/chest_pain_triage.drawio and /home/ga/Desktop/chest_pain_triage.png. The Drawio file must contain at least 2 pages, a minimum of 15 total shapes, and at least 3 diamond (decision) shapes. Page 1 must contain terminal nodes for 'discharge' and 'admit' (or similar outcomes) and be color-coded Red/Orange/Green.
